ab: maki su kayan ti 2022 (sazing siyan mapusan sazing kayan), kiya nakaygi Sefu ka minbunsyu Yencumin balay siyun baniru su lalu na Tayan la.
ch: 在2022年，政府召開了原住民身分證可單列族名的會議。
en: In 2022, the government announced that indigenous identification cards can include tribal names separately.

ab: pinusan mu ku basiya masina ki Sefu sasiyan lalu Tayan ku minbunsyu ka sining alang Noami Kulas.
ch: 我訪問了長期向政府爭取身分證「單列族名」的部落青年Noami Kulas。
en: I interviewed Noami Kulas, a young tribal member who has long been advocating for the inclusion of tribal names on identification cards.

ab: minkayan kung ki hiya masatana’a ta ti pahangi’an hani ga musa kung ma’ut ti sali niya.
ch: 我和他約定好這個假日到他家訪問他。
en: We had arranged to visit his home this holiday for the interview.

ab: maki sa ma’ut hani ga, galuwnan kung niya pinayu’ ku inkahulan lalu niya, ru pasaba niya uziy ku mayanux si sali ki alang niya.
ch: 在訪問的過程中，他向我介紹了他的名字的由來，也帶我認識了他的家人、他的部落。
en: During the interview, he introduced to me the origin of his name and also introduced me to his family and tribe.

ab: pa’usan mu hiya maha huwa su sapawzit sa’ayung ku a’iyu lalu hani, baniru su sarali ga ya’ih pi?
ch: 我問他為什麼堅持要改名，舊的登記方式不好嗎?
en: I asked him why he insisted on changing his name. Wasn’t the old registration method sufficient?

ab: sawun kung niya maha, ya’igi sakahun su lalu cyukuk ga ini niya patahakiy sa’ayung kakusa na lalu niya, mutu naha sawun maha 「那先生」, misa su mutu ya’ani ku sapasiya naha uziy ma.
ch: 他對我說，因為用中文字並不能準確的傳達他的名字，其他人還會叫他「那先生」，也因此鬧出不少笑話。
en: He told me that using Chinese characters could not accurately convey his name, and others would still call him \"Na Xian Sheng,\" which resulted in many jokes being made.

ab: maha Noami Kulas, “baniru lalu su kayi na Yencumin hani ku payang ta lalu balay, niwan na inkahulan nakisan Atayan, ru utux sali nabakis, lalu nanak saxa’ mayanux hani ga, muwa na saluwan hmkani sabanahan nanak kwara lalu naha ki.”
ch: Noami Kulas說：「原住民語書寫的名字才是我們真正的名字。我的名字代表了我們的族群、我們的家族、我們的身分，希望能有更多族人找回自己的名字。」
en: Noami Kulas said, \"The names written in indigenous languages are our true names. My name represents our ethnic group, our family, our identity. I hope more fellow tribespeople can reclaim their own names.\"
